name: Mirador de Ikuspegia and Bosque Encantado de Urbasa – circular through Urbasa-Andia Natural Park
name_original: Mirador de Ikuspegia y Bosque Encantado de Urbasa – circular por el Parque Natural Urbasa-Andia
region_intro: Hiking around Urbasa offers diverse landscapes, characterized by its karstic massif with limestone formations, extensive beech forests, and dramatic geological features. The region features the striking turquoise pools of the Urederra River's source and panoramic viewpoints like the Balcón de Pilatos. The Sierra de Andía presents a rugged, rocky landscape with higher elevations. This varied terrain provides a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Urbasa The most popular hiking route is Mirador de Ikuspegia and Bosque Encantado de Urbasa – circular through Urbasa-Andia Natural Park, a 6.4 miles (10.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads through unique geological formations and atmospheric beech woods. Another top favourite among local hikers is San Adrián Hermitage – Portupekoleze Arch loop from Lizarragako mendatea, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.9 km) path. This loop connects historical sites with natural arches and offers varied scenery. Local hikers also love the Cheese – Source of the Urederra loop from Eltxoen iturria, a 3.5 miles (5.6 km) trail leading through the stunning turquoise pools and waterfalls of the Urederra River's source, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Hiking around Urbasa is defined by karstic terrain, dense beech forests, and dramatic river sources. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Urbasa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1200 hikers have used komoot to explore Urbasa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Urbasa?
A: Urbasa offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 50 routes documented on komoot. These range from easy strolls to more challenging ascents, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Urbasa?
A: The trails in Urbasa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from dense beech forests to dramatic karstic formations, and the stunning natural attractions like the Source of the Urederra River.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Urbasa?
A: Urbasa is beautiful year-round. Autumn is particularly popular for the vibrant colors of the beech forests. Summer offers refreshing shade under the canopy, while spring brings lush greenery. Even in winter, specific routes can be enjoyed, though conditions may require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there trails that feature waterfalls or natural pools in Urbasa?
A: Yes, the most famous natural attraction is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1390836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Urederra River&lt;/a&gt;, known for its stunning turquoise pools and waterfalls. You can experience this on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15002812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cheese – Source of the Urederra loop from Eltxoen iturria&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.5-mile trail.

Q: Can I find trails through the famous beech forests of Urbasa?
A: Absolutely. Urbasa is renowned for its extensive and often 'enchanted' beech forests. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964898053/mirador-de-ikuspegia-and-bosque-encantado-de-urbasa-circular-through-urbasa-andia-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Ikuspegia and Bosque Encantado de Urbasa – circular through Urbasa-Andia Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through these atmospheric woods and unique geological formations.

Q: Are there any viewpoints with panoramic scenery in Urbasa?
A: Yes, the region offers breathtaking panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964898053/mirador-de-ikuspegia-and-bosque-encantado-de-urbasa-circular-through-urbasa-andia-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Ikuspegia&lt;/a&gt;, part of a popular circular route, provides expansive vistas. Additionally, the Balcón de Pilatos (Ubaba Viewpoint) offers dramatic views over the Urederra River's source and the Améscoa Valley.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Urbasa?
A: Yes, Urbasa has several routes suitable for families. The path along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1390836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Urederra River&lt;/a&gt; is particularly popular, featuring walkways and bridges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15002812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cheese – Source of the Urederra loop from Eltxoen iturria&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.5-mile trail, ideal for a family outing.

Q: What kind of unique geological features can I expect to see in Urbasa?
A: Urbasa is a karstic massif, meaning you'll encounter fascinating limestone formations. These include spectacular caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3006763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Los Cristinos&lt;/a&gt;, ravines, dolines, and natural arches such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3065525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portupekoleze Arch&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2890218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zalamportillo Arch&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10523126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Adrián Hermitage – Portupekoleze Arch loop from Lizarragako mendatea&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to explore some of these features.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Urbasa?
A: Yes, many trails in Urbasa are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964898053/mirador-de-ikuspegia-and-bosque-encantado-de-urbasa-circular-through-urbasa-andia-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Ikuspegia and Bosque Encantado de Urbasa circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through diverse landscapes.

Q: What is the highest point in Urbasa-Andía Natural Park?
A: The highest point in the Sierra de Andía, part of the natural park, is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5548584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Donato Summit&lt;/a&gt;, reaching 1,489 meters. It is crowned with the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3379327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Donato&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive panoramas.

Q: Are there any historical sites or hermitages along the trails?
A: Yes, the region combines natural beauty with historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10523126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Adrián Hermitage – Portupekoleze Arch loop from Lizarragako mendatea&lt;/a&gt; connects historical sites with natural arches, offering varied scenery and a glimpse into the area's heritage.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Urbasa?
A: While Urbasa-Andía Natural Park is a natural habitat for diverse wildlife, dogs are generally permitted on trails, but it is crucial to keep them on a leash to protect the local fauna and flora. Always check specific trail regulations before you go.
tips: With a very special charm to which it owes its name, this beautiful and picturesque Beech forest is an unforgettable walk that can be enjoyed by the whole family. Depending on the route you choose, you can make a short or long circular route ascending to Bargagain peak or just go and return along the same path to the forest. Whichever route you choose, this place will surprise you with its beauty and picturesque surroundings.

One of the points of interest along the route through the enchanted forest. This curious rock formation with its peak at 1,133 meters above sea level offers wonderful views of the valley and the Urbasa-Andia Park that make it worth the physical effort to get there.
A pass of views, it is worth the effort.

If you have come this far, the only thing left to do is contemplate the wonderful panoramic views from this viewpoint. You will be able to contemplate the entire valley to one side and the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park to the other with a superior view of the picturesque enchanted forest.
